evidence.claim: Taking a patient removes them from the queue.
evidence.fact: Taking a patient removes them from the queue.
evidence.fact_span: Taking a patient removes them from the queue.

evidence.claim: The system provides the patient at the top of the queue.
evidence.fact: The system lets a physician take the patient at the head of the queue.
evidence.fact_span: The system lets a physician take the patient at the head of the queue.

evidence.claim: The physician has access to the queue system.
evidence.fact: The system lets a physician take the patient at the head of the queue.
evidence.fact_span: The system lets a physician take the patient at the head of the queue.

evidence.claim: The queue is sorted by patient urgency.
evidence.fact: The queue sorts patients primarily by their urgency level, and secondarily by arrival time within that level.
evidence.fact_span: The queue sorts patients primarily by their urgency level, and secondarily by arrival time within that level.

evidence.claim: The system allows the physician to take the next patient from the top of the sorted queue.
evidence.fact: When a doctor is ready to see a patient, they pull the person at the top of the list, which clears them from the queue.
evidence.user_story_span: take the next patient from the top of the sorted queue
evidence.fact_span: pull the person at the top of the list

evidence.claim: When the interaction occurs, the physician requests the next patient.
evidence.fact: A physician can take the next patient from the front of the queue, removing that person from the waiting list.
evidence.user_story_span: When the physician requests the next patient
evidence.fact_span: take the next patient from the front of the queue
